level_signals: 10+ years of industry product management experience
Expected to own product strategy, multi-quarter roadmaps, and cross-functional alignment at scale

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of industry product management experience (does not include internships nor includes co-ops)
Experience in payments, fintech, financial infrastructure, or a closely adjacent domain (e.g., commerce platforms, banking, payment networks) and a track record of building highly impactful products
Demonstrated experience partnering closely with engineers, designers, and external partners to build products and complex systems at scale
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with a knack for precise and concise articulation of user problems

Strong analytical capabilities with experience defining and tracking success metrics, running experiments (e.g., holdback tests), and using data to measure product impact on revenue, conversion, and margins
Experience working on products that require aligning multiple stakeholders, developing joint roadmaps, and navigating competing priorities between internal goals and external partner objectives
Ability to thrive in a dynamic and fast-paced environment with significant autonomy and responsibility
Background delivering successfully on multi-quarter roadmaps and aligning and influencing diverse stakeholders to achieve shared goals
Strong business acumen and comfort with complex ecosystem and platform-level problems

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree (or relevant degree equivalent): Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Analytics, Mathematics, Physics, Applied Sciences
Demonstrated success and strong execution track record in building payment products
Experience working at scale — you've managed products handling high transaction volumes where availability, latency, and marginal basis points matter

scope.surface: Stripe Payments organization — including payment processing APIs, regional/non-card payment options, in-person payments, Optimized Checkout, and Link
scope.business_model: B2B financial infrastructure platform (API-first, revenue from payment processing fees)
core_responsibilities: Develop the long-term vision and strategy for your pillar and create and execute on a compelling roadmap
Build deep user empathy with both Stripe's merchants and financial partners, understanding global payment industry trends and competitors' offerings to influence Stripe's roadmap effectively
Collaborate with engineering, design, legal, and other teams to jointly shape product experiences that delight Stripe's users
Ensure we are building a reliable, performant platform, including system reliability, API extensibility, and latency
Use metrics to inform your point of view and leverage analytics to measure success

green_flags: Clear product scope within a well-known payments platform (Stripe)
Explicit team-matching process described, allowing flexibility across subteams
Encouraged to apply if minimum requirements are met — 'preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement'
High ownership: 'own a critical piece of Stripe's payments stack'
Global scale and real revenue impact clearly articulated
red_flags: Location and work-auth requirements entirely absent from JD — remote eligibility, office requirements, and visa sponsorship are all unclear
Team matching is deferred to final stages — candidates may not know which specific subteam they are interviewing for until late in the process
10+ year minimum is a strict bar with no equivalency language offered

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff' and 10+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as metrics)
location.type (not stated in JD body; defaulted to 'unclear')
ambiguities: Location type is entirely unstated — no city, hybrid/remote/onsite policy, or work authorization requirements appear anywhere in the JD body
Team matching is explicitly deferred: 'exact team matching for one of the subteams within this org will begin during final stages' — candidates may also be considered for different orgs entirely
The 'preferred qualifications' section lists a STEM degree which could be read as a soft requirement, but the framing explicitly states preferred qualifications are 'a bonus, not a requirement'
